<!-- gitloco-start: do not edit between these markers — regenerated by `gitloco --install-mcp` -->
## GitLoco review

This repo uses [GitLoco](https://github.com/) for AI-code review. The human
leaves comments on diff lines through a local browser UI; you reach them
through the `gitloco` MCP server (declared in `.mcp.json`).

When the human asks you to "address review comments," "fix what I flagged,"
or similar:

- Call `list_open_threads` to get every open thread, **ordered oldest commit
  first** — work through them in that order.
- For each thread call `get_thread` for full context (replies, the file
  snapshots the human was looking at, `all_files` for the whole commit,
  `history_since`, `working_tree_patch`, `current_content`).
- If the comment is a clarifying question, post a reply with
  `reply_to_thread` and move on.
- If it's a code change, apply it **inside the original commit** via
  interactive rebase (`git rebase -i <commit>^`, set the target to `edit`,
  amend, `git rebase --continue`), then `reply_to_thread` describing what
  changed.
- **Do not** call any resolve tool — there isn't one. Humans resolve
  threads in the UI.

For the full procedure, see `.claude/commands/gitloco.md`
(slash-command `/gitloco`).
<!-- gitloco-end -->
